### Post by robitor on 2007-07-21
hi

please help me get my ati radeon x1300 working with flgrx.

### Post by strabes on 2007-07-25
Here's my xorg.conf (I have the X1400 working perfectly with fglrx): You can get rid of all that wacom nonsense as long as you don't have a tablet PC. Just make sure you remove it from the serverlayout section as well. ```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbVariant" "dvorak"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "SHMConfig" "true"
	Option	    "MinSpeed" ".1"
	Option	    "MaxSpeed" ".3"
	Option	    "AccelFactor" ".0017"
	Option	    "MaxTapTime" "150"
	Option	    "MaxDoubleTapTime" "150"
	Option	    "VertEdgeScroll" "1"
	Option	    "HorizEdgeScroll" "1"
	Option	    "VertScrollDelta" "300"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "250"
	Option	    "BottomEdge" "4300"
	Option	    "RightEdge" "5250"
	Option	    "TapButton2" "2"
	Option	    "TapButton3" "3"
	Option	    "RTCornerButton" "0"
	Option	    "LTCornerButton" "0"
#right click
	Option	    "RBCornerButton" "3"
#middle click
	Option	    "LBCornerButton" "2"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

display: :0.0  screen: 0

OpenGL renderer string: ATI Mobility Radeon X1400

### Post by DestroyMicroshaft on 2007-09-13
I know this subject has been dead for a few weeks but, I had the same problem with my x1300, that is until yesterday! The new ATI driver fixes this issue, worked perfectly for me.

Heres a link to the new driver! [http://www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/at...x86.x86_64.run]("http://www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/at...x86.x86_64.run")

If anyone needs help with this let me know, its pretty basic tho, install the driver via terminal, run in terminal >  aticonfig --initial and reboot, make sure not to enable your driver in the restricted driver manager!!! If anyone needs any help just shout!:mrgreen:

### Post by jd_cincy on 2007-10-19
Dell E521 with Radeon 1300 Pro

Before you start screwing with anything, get a working session (no black screen) after an install and backup your xorg.conf file (I posted a working one in a previous reply):
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup

Ok, got compiz working...  here's what I had to do:
1. Download newest drivers from ATI.com and install:
sudo sh ati-driver-installer-8.40.4-x86.x86_64.run

2. Add a skip check to the compiz manager file (make a backup first):
sudo gedit /etc/xdg/compiz/compiz-manager
Add the following to the end:  SKIP_CHECKS="yes"

3. Follow the Feisty Guide to setting up an XGL session here:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Beryl/ATI/Feisty](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Beryl/ATI/Feisty)

Do NOT follow step-by step... you have to skip some things, namely -
gksudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
deb [http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/](http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/) feisty main
sudo wget [http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg](http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg) -O- | sudo apt-key add -


Also, when you get to the following step:
sudo apt-get install xserver-xgl beryl-ubuntu beryl-manager

Change it to:
sudo apt-get install xserver-xgl

Once all this is done, CRTL-ALT-Backspace out to a login prompt and select a one time XGL session as your login (bottom-left corner).  You will get an error message once you log in that says you don't need to use a XGL specific session and to resume your normal session (which is why I said use a one-time XGL session above).

Login and enjoy...

Following these steps got me up and running with Compiz.  Hopefully this saves a couple people the 4 hours it took me!

EDIT:  One thing that drove me nuts for a few minutes was once I changed from desktop wall to desktop cube, it was still a wall.  You gotta right-click your desktops down in the bottom-right and change to at least 4 (cubes with 2 sides are.... well.. a wall).  There is package called Compiz manager in package manager that makes this configuration very easy.

JD

---

### Post by jd_cincy on 2007-10-21
I hadn't screwed with anything yet, just made sure it rebooted without issue a couple times and made a backup copy of the xorg.conf.

Sorry I don't have a better answer for that....

---
